zotero.abstractNote: Feature visualization is a popular technique to interpret the internal representations of convolutional neural networks (CNNs). It typically involves generating synthetic images that maximize the activation of specific neurons. However, it remains unclear whether these synthetic visualizations are representative of the features that neurons actually respond to in natural images. In this work, we systematically compare synthetic feature visualizations with natural images that maximize neuron activations. We find that natural images are more informative for interpreting CNN activations than state-of-the-art synthetic feature visualizations. Our results suggest that synthetic visualizations may be biased towards features that are not representative of the natural image statistics that CNNs are trained on.
